items.title: Cambodia's informal workers, gig economy feel the pinch amid volatile global energy prices
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: Thousands of workers driving Cambodia's informal sector and the gig economy are feeling the pinch following the surge in fuel prices. Workers in the informal sector and the gig economy, including food stall vendors, auto-rickshaw drivers, last-mile food delivery services, and motor taxis, are beginning to suffer financial hardship due to costly fuels. Diesel prices rose to 8,100 riel (RM8.19) per litre, regular petrol (Gasoline 92) costs 5,500 riel (RM5.05) per litre, and LPG costs 3,900 riel (RM3.94) per litre.

items.title: Selangor rolls out WFH for civil servants, reviews Budget 2026 as energy shock triggers crisis task force
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — Selangor will review its 2026 state Budget to prioritise cost-of-living relief, support for affected industries and a faster energy transition, as it moves to cushion the fallout from a widening energy crisis linked to the US–Israel war against Iran. To accelerate its response, the state will also set up a Selangor Energy Crisis Special Task Force comprising experts from socio-economic, industrial, energy, food supply, banking and academic fields to ensure measures are “comprehensive and appropriate”. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the steps were agreed at a special meeting of the Selangor Economic Action Council.

items.title: High Court in Melaka orders Mas Ermieyati to pay RM50,000 in damages and apologise to MARA, Asyraf Wajdi over defamatory statements
items.summary: MELAKA, April 6 — The Melaka High Court has ordered Masjid Tanah Member of Parliament Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin to pay RM50,000 in damages for making defamatory statements against Majlis Amanah Rakyat and its chairman, Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, last year. Lawyer Adnan Seman @ Abdullah, representing Asyraf Wajdi, said the court also ordered Mas Ermieyati to issue a public apology within 14 days and to withdraw three videos and related statements posted on social media that defamed MARA and his client. Mas Ermieyati was represented by lawyer Zulfahami Abu Bakar.

items.title: Perkeso consults stakeholders on commuter scheme for Malaysians working in Singapore
items.summary: SINGAPORE, April 6 — The Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) on Monday held its final stakeholder engagement session in Singapore ahead of the implementation of the commuter scheme, scheduled for the second quarter of this year. Perkeso group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said the session involved key stakeholders such as 1 Third Media Movement; the Singapore Accident Help Centre (Johor); the Malaysian Association in Singapore (MASIS); and representatives of employers in Singapore. Also present at the engagement session was Malaysia’s High Commissioner to Singapore, Datuk Dr Azfar Mohamad Mustafar.

items.title: Nurul Izzah slams PN's motorcycle convoy during energy crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR deputy president Nurul Izzah Anwar criticised Perikatan Nasional's Saturday motorcycle convoy as tone-deaf and wasteful amid the energy crisis. She said that as the world grapples with fragile energy supply chains due to the escalating conflict in Middle East, governments worldwide are responding urgently by tightening consumption, building buffers and preparing their economies for prolonged uncertainty. "Yet here at home, we witness a motorcycle convoy involving thousands.

items.title: Minimum Wage Order 2024 review slated for completion by September, says deputy minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 6 — The review of the Minimum Wage Order 2024 is expected to be completed this September, Deputy Human Resources Minister Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan said. He said the findings of the study would serve as the primary basis for deciding whether to revise or retain the current minimum wage rate. Khairul Firdaus said the government remained committed to safeguarding the welfare of all parties and assured that any decision regarding the adjustment of the Minimum Wage Order would not affect the development and growth of the employment ecosystem.

items.title: Veteran journalist Karam Singh Walia laid to rest at Simpang Lima crematorium
items.summary: KLANG, April 6 — The remains of veteran broadcast journalist Datuk Karam Singh Walia were laid to rest in a cremation ceremony at the Simpang Lima Crematorium here at about 4pm today. Among those present to pay their last respects were Media Prima Berhad Group chairman Datuk Seri Dr Syed Hussian Syed Junid, Media Prima On-Air Presentation general manager Kamaruddin Mape, and Media Prima Group deputy managing editor of News and Current Affairs Farrah Naz Abd Karim. Karam Singh, 67, died at 3.30am today at his residence, as confirmed by his younger brother Jasbant Singh, who is also Group Managing Editor of News and Current Affairs at Media Prima Berhad.

items.title: Four-day cloud-seeding op starts today to raise dam water levels
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government will implement cloud-seeding operations from today until Thursday to raise water levels at major dams and reduce the impact of the current hot weather in several states. Natural Resources and Environment Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the measure is being taken following the weather status report as of yesterday, which showed that Kota Setar in Kedah is currently at Level 2 (heat wave), with daily maximum temperature exceeding 37 to 40°C for at least three consecutive days. He said several areas in Perlis, Kedah, Kelantan, Perak, Pahang and Johor have also been placed under Level 1 (alert) status following daily maximum temperatures of between 35 and 37°C for three consecutive days.

items.title: MetMalaysia to run tsunami siren tests in Penang tomorrow, public told to keep calm and carry on
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — Penang residents have been urged not to panic ahead of tsunami warning siren tests scheduled across the state tomorrow. In a statement, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the tests will be carried out at three sites: the Tanjung Bungah Floating Mosque from 9am to 10am, followed by Masjid Ar-Rahman in Batu Ferringhi from 11am to noon, and the Malaysian Fisheries Development Authority (LKIM) in Teluk Bahang from 2pm to 3pm. The exercise is part of ongoing preparedness efforts to ensure the tsunami early warning system remains in good working order and fully operational.

items.title: Fuziah: Party's strength lies in shared values, not just structure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR can no longer operate solely as a functional organisation but must evolve into a cultured organisation. Its secretary-general, Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh, said that while the party previously focused on strengthening its structure, the emphasis now must shift to building the organisation's culture. "Our president (Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim) stressed that the real strength of the party lies not just in its organisational structure, but in the values we uphold together.

items.title: Mas Ermieyati issues apology to Mara chairman, to pay RM50,000
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Masjid Tanah member of parliament Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin has admitted in court to making false and defamatory statements against Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki and the institution, and has been ordered to pay RM50,000 in damages. The Melaka High Court, presided over by Judicial Commissioner Isa Aziz Ibrahim, recorded a consent judgment against Mas Ermieyati, who agreed to issue a written apology, retract her statements, and delete all related video posts across her social media platforms. Asyraf, who announced the outcome on Facebook, said that the RM50,000 in damages would not be used for personal gain, saying he had instructed Mara management to channel the full sum into the Mara Waqf fund upon receipt, for the benefit of poor, underprivileged, and orphaned students.

items.title: AmBank extends RM2bil facility to Weststar Aviation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AMMB Holdings Bhd (AmBank), via AmBank Islamic has extended RM2 billion financing to Weststar Aviation Services Sdn Bhd to support fleet expansion and operations. AmBank group chief executive officer Jamie Ling said the bank supports Weststar's expansion and global ambitions. Weststar chairman and group managing director Tan Sri Dr Syed Azman Syed Ibrahim said the financing will accelerate fleet expansion and operational capabilities.

items.title: Splash back into adventure: Mersing reopens island tours after monsoon break
items.summary: MERSING, April 6 — All scuba diving, snorkelling, and island tour activities in the waters off Mersing have officially resumed as of last Saturday, marking the end of the North-east Monsoon season. The Mersing District Council, via its official Facebook page MY Mersing, stated that the district’s tourism sector is now fully operational and ready to receive visitors in conjunction with the 2026 Visit Johor Year. However, authorities emphasised that a “carrying capacity” approach will be enforced to ensure sustainable tourism.

items.title: Karam's last night: A final hug for his 'brother' Yazzer [WATCH]
items.summary: KLANG: When Mohammad Yazzer Mahmun arrived at Datuk Karam Singh Walia's house last night, an unfamiliar heaviness settled over him, a sadness he had never felt in two decades of knowing the man. In Yazzer's eyes, Karam was never merely a colleague. Karam, 67, died at 3.30am this morning.

items.title: CAP urges swift action over Sungai Marong pollution
items.summary: The association said the incident, which saw the river water turning black and foamy, was alarming and demanded urgent intervention. (Hyperlink: https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/04/1409305/black-foamy-sungai-marong-jelebu-kills-fish) Jelebu Fisheries Department chief Mohd Azhar Jusoh said the Fisheries Department had investigated complaints from local fishermen and confirmed that the river water was black and foamy. CAP president Mohideen Abdul Kader said stern enforcement action must be taken against the culprits.

items.title: Club riot leave Cheras traders shaken
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The commotion at a Taman Shamelin Perkasa entertainment centre on Saturday left traders shaken, but business is back to normal. Checks at the scene showed no visible police presence, with patrons continuing to fill nearby eateries. Another trader operating near the premises said she was similarly shocked.

items.title: Ukraine regains control of frontline areas in southeast and east, army chief says
items.summary: KYIV: Ukraine has regained control of 480 sq. Despite Ukraine's successes, Russian troops were pressing ahead with a spring offensive, he said. The Ukrainian troops maintained defence lines, he said.

items.title: Man behind ‘N13 Kuang’ account admits false claim against Fahmi over mosque speech
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — The defamation lawsuit filed by Datuk Fahmi Fadzil against the Facebook account owner N13 Kuang, Mohd Fauzan Madzlan, in 2023 over allegations that the communications minister delivered a political speech at a mosque in Rawang, Selangor, was settled at the High Court here today. Fahmi’s lawyer, Fahri Azzat, informed Justice Datuk Raja Ahmad Mohzanuddin Shah Raja Mohzan that both parties had reached a settlement and agreed to a consent judgment. The matter was confirmed by Mohd Fauzan’s lawyer, Mohammed Zamri Ibrahim.

items.title: Karam Singh Walia died calmly, surrounded by family, says son
items.summary: KLANG: Despite battling liver cancer for the past two years, Datuk Karam Singh Walia spent his final moments with his family in good spirits before dying early today. The journalist's second son, Commander Malvinderjit Singh Walia, said that before taking his last breath at 3.30am, his father had spent time with family and friends at home after being discharged from hospital yesterday. A multiple Malaysia Press Institute award winner, Karam won the Best Television Environmental Journalist category in 2004, 2005, 2006 and 2007.

items.title: Penang moves to evict ineligible PPR tenants as waiting list tops 1,000
items.summary: State Housing and Environment Committee chairman Datuk Seri S. Sundarajoo said about 30 per cent of existing occupants in Penang’s PPR and State Rental Housing (RSKN) units are no longer eligible to continue living there. He said tenants who have overstayed or exceeded income eligibility under PPR and RSKN guidelines will need to vacate the units to make way for others. PPR arrears total RM254,633 involving 268 units, while RSKN arrears total RM1.77 million involving 907 units.

items.title: AirAsia trims capacity, tightens costs and raises fares amid fuel pressures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AirAsia X Bhd is adjusting capacity, tightening costs and adjusting fares to mitigate rising fuel prices, said its co-founder and advisor Tan Sri Tony Fernandes. He said the airline is leveraging its experience from past crises, including the Covid-19 pandemic, to navigate the current challenges, stressing that demand for air travel remains resilient despite rising costs. He said AirAsia's cargo business continues to see robust demand, supported by its logistics arm Teleport.

items.title: AGC says Sabah 40pc formula untouched as court freezes payment timeline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — The Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC) today stressed that the government’s appeal in the Sabah revenue dispute does not challenge the state’s 40 per cent entitlement, after the Court of Appeal granted a stay pausing payment deadlines and negotiations tied to the long-running case. In a statement today, the AGC said the stay “does not cover the High Court’s findings regarding the 40 per cent entitlement under Article 112C of the Federal Constitution”. The clarification came after the Court of Appeal in Putrajaya allowed the federal government’s application to suspend the execution of a Kota Kinabalu High Court judgment pending appeal.

items.title: Man jailed 3 days, fined RM6,000 for driving against traffic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A motorist who went viral for driving against the flow of traffic along Jalan Klang Lama two days ago was sentenced to three days' imprisonment and fined RM6,000 after pleading guilty in court today. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the local man admitted to the charge and settled the fine. Earlier, it was reported that the driver, whose actions went viral for driving against traffic along Jalan Klang Lama here, had turned himself in before being detained by police last night.

items.title: Minister: Four-day cloud seeding launched as heatwave grips Malaysia, temperatures hit 40°C
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — Malaysia will begin cloud seeding operations this week as parts of the country swelter under heatwave conditions, with temperatures climbing as high as 40°C in some areas. Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the operations, led by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), will run from today until April 9 in collaboration with the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) and the Royal Malaysian Air Force. Kurup said the cloud seeding operations are intended to boost water levels in key reservoirs while also helping to moisten ecosystems.

items.title: Allegations against Selangor health director unfounded - Health Ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Health Ministry has dismissed allegations made against the Selangor health director as baseless, following a report published by an online news portal on April 2. In a statement today, the ministry said a Complaints Investigation Committee had been established at its Putrajaya headquarters to ensure an objective review of the matter. "The investigation found that the allegations were unfounded," it said, adding that the ministry remained committed to ensuring all operational, administrative and management actions complied with existing regulations and guidelines.

items.title: Myanmar's new president Min Aung Hlaing faces genocide complaint in Indonesia
items.summary: JAKARTA: A criminal case against Myanmar's newly-elected President Min Aung Hlaing was filed in Indonesia on Monday by a group of civil society organisations, accusing the leader of acts of genocide against the Rohingya ethnic group. Indonesia, which hosts Asean's headquarters, is the biggest Muslim majority country in the world, and is among the destinations for Rohingya people fleeing Myanmar or the refugee camps by boat. Myanmar has always denied genocide.

items.title: ‘Marks had to be 90pc and above’: Son recalls veteran journalist Karam Singh Walia’s firm but loving guidance
items.summary: KLANG, April 6 — The late Datuk Karam Singh Walia was remembered as a loving yet firm father who instilled the importance of knowledge in his children, particularly the mastery of languages. His second son, Commander Melvinderjit Singh Walia, 42, recalled that whenever he or his siblings used inaccurate words or phrases at home, their father would immediately reprimand them and demand that the error be corrected on the spot. The deceased’s younger brother, Jasbant Singh, said that although Karam Singh never received a formal linguistic education, he carved his own niche by using language with refinement, incorporating pantun and proverbs into his reporting.

items.title: Hungarians' growing anger at living in EU's 'most corrupt state'
items.summary: HUNGARIAN leader Viktor Orban's officially declared wealth is fairly modest: some savings and a jointly owned villa in Budapest. But voters in what Transparency International deems the EU's most corrupt country believe otherwise. Independent lawmaker Akos Hadhazy, one of Hungary's leading anti-corruption crusaders, said graft has drained the equivalent of 2.84 billion euros (US$3.27 billion) from state coffers every year since 2016.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: Malaysia stepping up refugee management to safeguard security
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 6 — The government will continue to strengthen its management of refugees and asylum seekers through enhanced inter-agency coordination to ensure national security remains safeguarded, said Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. Chairing a meeting for the Security Cluster Working Committee on the Management of Refugees and Asylum Seekers today, he said Malaysia’s approach to the issue is being reviewed as it becomes increasingly complex and requires continuous attention. “The meeting focused on efforts to reinforce coordination among agencies while ensuring national security is preserved, alongside implementing a more structured and effective management of the matter,” he said in a post on his official Facebook page.

items.title: Hungary prepares to vote as Orban's future hangs in balance
items.summary: BUDAPEST: EU member Hungary will hold a closely watched general election on Sunday, the first since nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban's return to power in 2010 where he is not considered the clear favourite. Surveys from independent pollsters suggest that opposition leader Peter Magyar's conservative TISZA party could be heading for a landslide victory. Pro-government institutions however forecast a win for Orban's Fidesz-KDNP coalition.

items.title: Sabah has begun training healthcare personnel for new heart institute
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah will be working with the National Heart Institute (IJN) to have its stand-alone heart hospital here. Present were Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Deputy Chief Minister and Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun, Women, Family and Community Development Datuk Minister Julita Mojungki, and IJN Sdn Bhd chairman Tan Sri Dr Noor Hisham Abdullah. Ideally, an IJN hospital requires a big building, the funding will come from the Sabah government, Yayasan Sabah and IJN.

items.title: Only one of Daim's children shows up at MACC HQ
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Only one of the late Tun Daim Zainuddin's children had arrived at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters to give her statement as of 2pm today. His other three children are Md Wira Dani, Muhammed Amir Zainuddin and Muhammed Amin Zainuddin. The commission said the notices were issued after repeated attempts to contact the children failed as of Feb 26.

items.title: Hajiji: Talks must continue ‘in good faith’ despite stay on Sabah’s 40pc share
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 6 — The Sabah government hopes its ongoing negotiations with the federal government over its 40 per cent revenue entitlement will continue despite today’s Court of Appeal decision to grant a stay of the October 17 High Court order, which had set a 180-day timeline for payment. Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said that although the state opposed the stay, it respected the court’s decision to grant it pending the federal government’s appeal, describing the move as “procedural” in nature. On October 17 last year, the Kota Kinabalu High Court ruled that the federal government had acted unlawfully by failing to honour Sabah’s right to 40 per cent of net federal revenue from 1974 to 2021.

items.title: King’s birthday celebrations begin with initiation ceremony in Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 6 — Celebrations for the official birthday of Malaysia’s King, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, began today with the ‘ Istiadat Meletak Kerja ’ (initiation) ceremony at Istana Besar, here. The flag-raising was preceded by a formal proclamation by Dato’ Penghulu Istiadat Datuk Obet Tawil, who held the ceremonial mace, and a prayer recital by Johor Mufti Yahya Ahmad. Among those present were Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, Johor Council of Royal Court president Datuk Abdul Rahim Ramli and State Secretary Datuk Mohammed Ridha Abd Kadir, along with senior state officials and heads of department.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues nationwide thunderstorm warning; heavy rain and strong winds expected until evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a weather alert for ten states and territories, warning of impending thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds expected to persist until 5.00pm this evening. According to the notice issued at 3.00pm, the affected areas in Peninsular Malaysia include the entire state of Perlis, as well as extensive parts of Kedah covering Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim, and Bandar Baharu. In Penang, the warning is in effect for Seberang Perai Utara, Tengah, and Selatan, while Perak sees alerts for Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang, and Muallim.

items.title: Govt outlines three key measures to tackle rising costs, support Bumiputera companies, says DPM Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 6 — The government has outlined three key measures to strengthen Bumiputera economic resilience, particularly in addressing global uncertainties arising from the prolonged geopolitical conflict in West Asia. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approach calls for firmer, faster and more targeted action through three measures, namely immediate and comprehensive assessment, targeted high-impact interventions, and firm, accountable implementation. 1/2026, which focused on strategic measures to strengthen Bumiputera economic resilience.

items.title: King's birthday celebrations begin with initiation ceremony in Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Celebrations for the official birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, began today with the Istiadat Meletak Kerja (initiation) ceremony at Istana Besar, here. According to a post on the monarch's official Facebook page, the ceremony was marked by the raising of the Johor government's ceremonial flag at 8.30am. Among those present were Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, Johor Council of Royal Court president Datuk Dr Abdul Rahim Ramli and State Secretary Datuk Mohammed Ridha Abd Kadir, along with senior state officials and heads of department.

items.title: Retired official charged with RM50,000 false claim
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A retired government official appeared in the Sessions Court here today, charged with using false documents to claim RM50,000 for repair work at Sri Sakthi Easwari Temple in Petaling, five years ago. The accused, M. Thiruselvam, 65, pleaded not guilty after the charge was read before judge Datuk Mohd Nasir Nordin. The invoice was allegedly handed to the 64-year-old temple secretary on Nov 22, 2021, at the temple premises.

items.title: Sabah calms rice shortage fears, blames panic buying for gaps
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 6 — Rice supplies in Sabah remain sufficient despite recent shortages reported at several major supermarkets in the Sepanggar area. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) and member of Parliament for Sepanggar, Datuk Mustapha Sakmud, said he is aware of public concerns but confirmed that monitoring shows rice stocks across the state are adequate. “I have obtained detailed updates from the Sabah office of Padiberas Nasional Berhad (Bernas) and the Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security regarding current rice reserves.

items.title: PN denies internal rifts, says focused on filling leadership spots
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has denied that there is any internal rift within the coalition, stressing that it is currently focused on strengthening and streamlining its leadership structure at both national and state levels. Takiyuddin said the issue should be understood as part of a broader move to consolidate and reinforce PN's organisational framework following recent leadership changes. He explained that apart from its national structure, PN also maintained leadership at the state level, which required updating following several resignations.

items.title: Karam's legacy will continue to inspire, says PM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has paid tribute to renowned environmental journalist Datuk Karam Singh Walia, who died today, saying his legacy will serve as an inspiration. "Karam was a highly respected broadcast journalist," Anwar said in a Facebook post. Karam, 67, died at his home in Klang at 3.30am.

items.title: Son recalls late Karam Singh Walia's discipline and love for language
items.summary: KLANG: The late Datuk Karam Singh Walia was a man of discipline and high standards, particularly when it came to language, his son said. Royal Malaysian Navy Commander Malvinderjit Singh Walia, the second son of the veteran journalist, recalled how his father would correct any mistakes in speech or pronunciation immediately. If we spoke incorrectly, he would correct us there and then," Melvinjeet said.

items.title: Anwar hails Karam Singh Walia’s contributions to journalism and environment after veteran journalist’s death
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has extended his condolences to the family of veteran broadcast journalist Datuk Karam Singh Walia, who died early this morning. In a Facebook post, Anwar described Karam Singh as a highly respected broadcast journalist. Karam Singh, 67, died at 3.30am today at his residence in Klang.

items.title: AirAsia X confident flying past June despite fuel concerns
items.summary: SEPANG: AirAsia X Bhd remains optimistic that it can continue operating flights beyond June 2026 despite concerns over jet fuel availability and rising costs due to the Middle Eastern crisis. Airlines globally have raised concern over potential jet fuel shortages and rising fuel prices, warning that the situation could affect their ability to continue operating flights. Philippine Airlines said it had secured sufficient jet fuel supply to support scheduled operations, including long-haul flights, for the foreseeable future.

items.title: Sabah orders destruction of koi fish after virus outbreak
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry ministry has ordered the immediate capture and destruction of all koi fish and non-endemic Cyprinidae species from tagal areas, fish farms and rivers across the state. Its minister, Jamawi Jaafar, said the directive followed confirmation that the Koi Herpesvirus (KHV), carried by the species, was the main cause of pelian (kelah) fish deaths reported in several districts since Aug 1, 2025. He said such irresponsible actions violated biosecurity principles and caused serious infections among local fish species, including pelian, which is classified as a high-value species.

items.title: Indonesia's budget deficit at end-March stood at 0.93pct of GDP, minister says
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's budget deficit in the first quarter of 2026 stood at 0.93 per cent of its gross domestic product, its finance minister said on Monday. Last year's Q1 budget deficit was only 0.41 per cent of GDP, and the increase this year was due to the frontloading of government spending. Purbaya said Q1 spending stood at 815 trillion rupiah (US$47.84 billion), up 31.4 per cent from the year before, while revenues hit 574.9 trillion rupiah, up 10.5 per cent from the first quarter of 2025.

items.title: ‘Make sure there is an answer’: Ipoh court gives police two more months to find Indira Gandhi’s ex-husband, daughter
items.summary: IPOH, April 6 — The High Court today granted police an additional two months to obtain crucial information on the recent activities of M. Indira Gandhi’s ex-husband, Muhammad Riduan Abdullah, as authorities remain unable to locate him and her youngest daughter, Prasana Diksa, who will turn 18 on April 8. Earlier, Federal Counsel Safiyyah Omar told the court that police had taken several steps to locate Muhammad Riduan. Safiyyah said police had examined closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage from a petrol station.

items.title: Thailand plans reforms to boost growth and cut business costs, PM draft statement says
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand will pursue broad economic and administrative reforms, aim for faster growth and cut business costs through the use of technology, according to a draft policy statement set to be delivered by the prime minister later this week. The statement also called for the fast-tracking of an omnibus law within the year to scrap outdated regulations that have held back businesses and growth. Last year, the Thai economy grew 2.4 per cent.

items.title: No special permits for KLCC photo touts, says Hannah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government will not consider issuing special permits to photo touts operating at tourist hotspots in the capital, citing safety concerns for tourists and road users. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the decision followed a site visit to the area where the photo touts operate near the Petronas Twin Towers. The police are also conducting integrated operations with DBKL," she said.

items.title: Gobind: Govt steps up monitoring of Middle East fallout, eyes digital sector impact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — The government, through a special committee under the National Economic Action Council (MTEN), will examine any issues arising from the conflict in West Asia that may affect sectors, particularly the digital sector. Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said the weekly MTEN meeting, to be chaired by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, will assess the potential effects of the conflict and formulate appropriate remedial measures. Gobind said the meeting will also bring together key ministries and industry stakeholders to coordinate responses, strengthen economic resilience, and ensure the livelihoods of Malaysians and the country’s businesses remain protected.

items.title: King grants audience to Chief Secretary to the Government
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar. Meanwhile, Shamsul Azri, in a Facebook post, extended his appreciation for the audience granted by Sultan Ibrahim. Shamsul Azri said he had presented the latest developments on federal development projects in Johor for His Majesty's attention.

items.title: Viral Old Klang Road wrong-way driver gets jail, RM6,000 fine and five-year licence suspension
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — A 32-year-old e-hailing driver who drove against the flow of traffic along Old Klang Road in Kuala Lumpur, in an act that went viral online, has been jailed for three days and fined RM6,000 after pleading guilty to reckless and dangerous driving. According to national daily The Star , Wong Kok Kong was sentenced by Magistrate Tg Syazwany Yasmyn Tuan Roslan at the Magistrate’s Court in Kuala Lumpur today under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987. Kuala Lumpur JSPT chief Asst Comm Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said investigations found the man had driven against traffic for about 500m after taking a wrong turn while heading towards the Mid Valley Megamall.

items.title: No more trips to KL? Sabah plans IJN-linked heart specialist centre in Likas
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 6 — Sabah will soon have its own heart specialist centre with the establishment of a National Heart Institute (IJN)-linked hospital in Likas, following the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) between the state government’s investment arm, Innoprise Corporation Sdn Bhd (ICSB), and IJN. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the move reflects the government’s commitment to improving access to quality healthcare. ICSB executive chairman Datuk Jasnih Daya said the hospital would reduce the need for Sabahans to seek treatment outside the state, lowering travel costs and enabling faster access to specialist care.

items.title: Retired civil servant, ex-temple chairman charged over RM50,000 forged invoice five years ago
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 6 — A retired civil servant and a former temple committee chairman were charged in separate Sessions Courts here today over a forged invoice for RM50,000 linked to consultancy services for temple repair works in the Petaling district five years ago. M Thiruselvam, 65, pleaded not guilty to the charge before Judge Datuk Mohd Nasir Nordin. He is accused of having dishonestly submitted the invoice for approval of repair works at a temple in Petaling, to a 64-year-old temple secretary on November 22, 2021, at the temple premises.

items.title: Army corporal pleads not guilty to assaulting seven fellow soldiers in Bentong
items.summary: Sukry Tasu, 37, entered the plea before Magistrate Muhammad Aiman Azahan. On the first charge, he is accused of causing grievous hurt to Muhammad Amirul Raziq Rosafindi, 24, at the armoury store of Company C, 25th Battalion Royal Malay Regiment Camp in Kem Bentong at 8.30am on March 30, 2026. Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Zamharir Muhammad Zuhid proposed bail of RM10,000, with an additional condition that the accused refrain from approaching or contacting the victim until the case is disposed of.

items.title: Sabah may review diesel subsidies if global energy crisis drags on, says Hajiji
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — A prolonged global energy crisis could impact Sabah’s economy, including a possible review of diesel subsidies if current conditions persist. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the impact would depend on developments in global conflicts, particularly those involving energy-producing countries such as Iran, according to a report published in Sinar Harian today. He said if the energy crisis and ongoing conflicts continue, they would not only pressure global energy markets but also affect Sabah’s economy.

items.title: Fresh charge for DOE deputy D-G over RM9,110 home repairs
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 6 — Department of Environment (DOE) deputy director-general (Development) Dr Norhazni Mat Sari appeared in the Shah Alam Sessions Court today over a new charge of accepting RM9,110 for home repairs in 2022. Deputy public prosecutor Muaz Ahmad Khairuddin requested the court to maintain Norhazni’s bail granted by the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court on April 3 and asked that this case be tried together with her previous charges, subject to the defence’s consent. Sessions Court Judge Datuk Mohd Nasir Nordin subsequently allowed the prosecution’s application for the case to be tried jointly and ordered that the bail and conditions imposed by the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court be maintained.

items.title: MCA to accord highest honour to Ling Liong Sik with coffin flag-draping ceremony tomorrow, says Wee Ka Siong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — A special ceremony will be held tomorrow to drape the coffin of the late Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik with the MCA party flag, an honour described as the highest tribute accorded to a leader of the party. MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong said the ceremony will take place at 4pm at the Xiao En Centre in Cheras, where family members, party leaders and members of the public have been paying their final respects to the former party president and transport minister. MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong with MCA party leaders at the wake of late Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik at at Xiao En Centre, Kuala Lumpur, on April 6, 2026.

items.title: MoE orders all Teacher Education Institute campuses to suspend water, adventure activities after two students drown
items.summary: IPOH, April 6 — The Ministry of Education (MoE) has ordered all Teacher Education Institute (IPG) campuses to temporarily suspend water and adventure activities following a drowning incident involving two students from the institute. Last Saturday, two students from IPG Perlis, Christopher Ling Jia Siang, 21, and Joey Ling Lin Siang, 20, were found drowned at about 7.30pm while participating in kayaking activities at Pantai Merdeka near Sungai Petani. “Police investigations are ongoing, and the MoE’s internal review is also underway.

items.title: Kedah Customs intercept RM3.9m worth of ecstasy and meth
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 6 — The Kedah Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) seized over 46kg of drugs during an operation on Jalan Kota Kuala Muda near Sungai Petani on February 21. State JKDM director Wan Marini Wan Hamzah said officers from the Langkawi and Tanjung Dawai Marine Enforcement Branches, acting on three months of intelligence and surveillance, tracked a sedan driven by a 40-year-old Indonesian man suspected of transporting illegal substances. The drugs were found in bags on the rear seat of the car,” he said during a press conference at the JKDM office here today.
